rulings.Case Title: PATRICK BURKE, ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder Of Defendant Csk Auto, Inc. To Defendant Cummins Engine Companys Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant CSK Auto, Inc.¿s joinder to defendant Cummins Engine Co.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs plaintiffs of this tentative ruling and they state that they do not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Chester C. Lehmann Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and loss of consortium and off calendar as to false representation. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not posses and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Breckler was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant did not include in its moving papers plaintiffs¿ responses to all of its special interrogatories, including the interrogatories asking for persons with knowledge of the facts supporting plaintiffs¿ claims, thus defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses. Further, Mr. Breckler¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Breckler¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing ¿asbestos wire¿ attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their false representation cause of action. = (220/HEK)
